data.title: Maple Finance Launches SyrupUSDG on Robinhood Chain, Approved as Collateral for Robinhood Earn Vault
data.description:
Maple Finance has launched a new product, syrupUSDG, and listed it on Robinhood Chain, with Steakhouse Financial approving syrupUSDG as collateral for the Robinhood Earn vault.

According to Foresight News, Robinhood Earn is described as the first decentralized lending product within the Robinhood app.

Under the collaboration, Morpho provides the vault infrastructure, Paxos is responsible for the compliant issuance of USDG, and Robinhood provides distribution to millions of users.

SyrupUSDG is currently available on Ethereum and Robinhood Chain. Maple Finance said it will support additional chains later, and Robinhood plans to roll out access to customers in phases over the coming weeks.

data.title: Claynosaurz Sets Aside 15% Equity Options for Eligible Community Members
data.description:
Claynosaurz, a Web3 entertainment project, said it has reserved 15% of its company equity to be distributed as options to eligible community members, including holders of all Claynosaurz digital collectibles such as Popkins.

According to Foresight News, the project said the allocation is intended to allow qualifying holders to share in returns tied to the company’s future growth.

Claynosaurz added that an allocation inquiry website will go live next week, where holders can check their eligible share and review detailed rules.

data.title: Dragonfly Partner Haseeb Says Crypto Industry Is Undergoing a Healthy Shakeout
data.description:
Dragonfly managing partner Haseeb said the crypto industry is not moving toward the middle and that some people leaving the sector reflects a filtering process he described as healthy.

According to Odaily, Haseeb wrote on X that this dynamic is similar to a normal cleanup after an early tech-industry bubble burst, leaving behind those who believe in long-term value. He said the process brings short-term pain but is positive over the long term.

Haseeb added that he remains bullish on ETH and SOL, saying he believes both have a significant future.

He also said crypto is essentially a continuation of technology and the internet, and he expects it to change the world in a way similar to the internet.

data.title: AI TRENDS | Saga Forms Saga AI Labs and Sells Crypto Business to dao5 Affiliate Alapin Holdings
data.description:
Blockchain company Saga has established Saga AI Labs to develop AI consumer platforms and autonomous digital characters, and it has sold its cryptocurrency business while transferring operational management rights for its blockchain network to Alapin Holdings, an affiliate of dao5. According to Foresight News, the company said the new lab will focus on AI-driven consumer products and autonomous digital roles.

Saga AI Labs has already implemented AI character projects with multiple gaming partners. The company said it plans to expand the lab’s business into areas including consumer brands and sports in the future.

data.title: Reflect Launches 180-Day Voluntary Recovery Plan for USDC+ Holders Affected by April Drift Hack
data.description:
Reflect, an a16z-backed stablecoin protocol, said it has started a separate voluntary recovery plan for USDC+ position holders impacted by Drift’s April hacking incident. According to ChainCatcher, the program opens a 180-day window starting now and allows holders to voluntarily sell their positions to Palindrome Engineering at 0.2 USDC plus 80 Reflect Credit (RC) per unit, with settlement conducted fully on-chain.

Reflect said the plan is pre-funded by Palindrome and is entirely independent of Drift’s recovery process. Participants who opt in must waive any claims against Drift in exchange for immediate, certain liquidity.

Holders who do not participate can continue to support Drift’s DFX recovery channel. Drift is now operating under the name Velocity, the report said.

data.title: WORLD CUP | Portugal face Croatia on anniversary of Diogo Jota and André Silva deaths
data.description: Portugal will play Croatia in a World Cup Round of 32 match Thursday night at Toronto's BMO Field on the one-year anniversary of the deaths of Portugal forward Diogo Jota and his brother André Silva, who were killed in a car crash in northern Spain. Defender Vitinha said the team has added motivation on the special day, while coach Roberto Martínez said Jota has been named an honorary member of the World Cup squad and the players are wearing wristbands with his name, according to Yahoo Sports.

data.title: Florida’s Brightline Uses Reserves to Make Key Bond Payments
data.description: Brightline, the debt-laden Florida private railroad, tapped reserves to make a July 1 interest payment due to holders of its senior municipal bonds and corporate notes, according to people familiar with the matter, Bloomberg reported.

data.title: Cumberland Farms Files for US IPO
data.description:
Cumberland Farms Ltd., the gas-station and convenience-store operator owned by EG Group, filed for a US initial public offering, according to Bloomberg.

The filing adds to a rush of debuts that has powered a record start to the year.

data.title: Danish Central Bank Intervenes to Defend Krone Peg After Slide
data.description: Denmark’s central bank intervened to support the krone and defend its peg to the euro after the currency weakened to its lowest level against the common currency in more than 25 years, according to Bloomberg.

data.title: STOCKS | US Stocks Give Up Jobs Report-Related Gains as Semis Pull Back
data.description:
US equities gave up earlier gains tied to the jobs report as semiconductor shares retreated, Bloomberg reported.

Technology stocks lagged the S&P 500 Index for a second straight day, extending a downturn after the rally in the previous quarter.

data.title: Goldman Sachs Says It Will Fund Eligible Employees’ Children’s “Trump Accounts”
data.description:
Goldman Sachs said it will provide funding support for “Trump accounts” for eligible employees’ children.

According to Jin10, the company said the support will be available to employees’ children who meet the eligibility requirements.

data.title: Onyx Protocol to Shut Down Onyx Layer 3 on December 31, 2026, Shifting Focus to Layer 1
data.description:
Onyx Protocol said it will deprecate and shut down Onyx Layer 3, also known as the XCN Ledger, on December 31, 2026, as it shifts development focus entirely to Onyx Layer 1.

According to Foresight News, Onyx said the transition will not change XCN itself.

The protocol said the Ethereum ERC-20 contract will remain the primary representation of XCN, and holders will continue to bridge assets between Ethereum and Onyx Layer 1 as needed.

As part of the transition, Onyx said XCN holders will be able to bridge their Ethereum-based XCN to Onyx Layer 1 to participate in staking, applications, and future network functions.

data.title: U.S. 8-Week Treasury Bill Auction Allocation Falls to 53.99%
data.description:
The U.S. 8-week Treasury bill auction allocation was 53.99% as of July 2.

According to Jin10, the previous reading was 70.5%.

data.title: STOCKS | Yimeng OncoBio Says HKEX Questions Loan Exemption; Independent Approval Needed
data.description:
Yimeng OncoBio Pharmaceutical (1541) said HKEX has questioned whether a partial exemption applies to a loan transaction involving its chairman, according to Ming Pao.

The company, which listed in September 2023, announced in February that it would provide a one-year loan of about RMB13.4247 million to chairman Tian Wenzhi for tax compliance purposes, with interest linked to the National Interbank Funding Center’s one-year quoted rate.

It said HKEX sent a letter on June 24 stating the partial exemption clause does not apply. HKEX said the transaction requires an independent financial adviser’s opinion and approval by independent shareholders.

data.title: Fhenix Acquires FHE Infrastructure Firm Sunscreen, Names Ravital Solomon Research Lead
data.description:
Fhenix, a fully homomorphic encryption (FHE) Layer 2 project, has acquired FHE infrastructure company Sunscreen for an undisclosed amount and appointed Sunscreen founder Ravital Solomon as its head of research. According to Foresight News, Fhenix said the acquisition is expected to strengthen its research and development capabilities in on-chain privacy computing.

Sunscreen focuses on applying FHE technology to blockchain and is described as an early participant in the sector. The company has also developed compiler technology within its ecosystem aimed at lowering the barrier for developers to use encrypted computation.

data.title: U.S. Bitcoin ETFs See Net Outflows While Ethereum ETFs Post Net Inflows, Lookonchain Says
data.description:
U.S. Bitcoin exchange-traded funds recorded net outflows of 6,165 BTC today, while U.S. Ethereum exchange-traded funds posted net inflows of 21,568 ETH, according to Lookonchain monitoring.

According to Odaily, Lookonchain data also showed Bitcoin ETFs had net outflows of 32,807 BTC over the past seven days.

Over the same seven-day period, Ethereum ETFs recorded net outflows of 54,411 ETH, the monitoring data showed.

data.title: AI | China’s Kling AI Raises $2 Billion to Expand AI Video Operations
data.description: China’s Kling AI raised an initial $2 billion in venture capital funding as the Kuaishou Technology spinoff seeks to expand its AI video operations, according to Bloomberg.

data.title: AI TRENDS | Cisco Launches Nexus One Data Center Network Architecture for AI Workloads
data.description:
Cisco has announced the launch of Cisco Nexus One, an open data center network architecture designed for AI workloads and new security threats, aimed at upgrading its long-evolving Application Centric Infrastructure (ACI) system.

According to Odaily, Cisco said Nexus One will connect to its unified platform, Cisco Cloud Control, to improve architectural flexibility, openness, and scalability while maintaining backward compatibility with existing systems.

Cisco added that Nexus One is not a single product but an architecture-level evolution similar to ACI. The company said the focus is shifting from early-stage innovation to standardization and interoperability across ecosystems to meet complex networking needs in the AI and machine learning era.

data.title: BOE’s Mann Says She’s Ready to Hike if Price Pressures Persist
data.description: Bank of England rate-setter Catherine Mann said she is prepared to make an “activist” interest-rate hike if inflation expectations and other warning signs on prices do not improve later this year, according to Bloomberg.

data.title: STOCKS | Fullwealth Holdings to Change Hands After Offer at HK$0.6545 a Share
data.description:
Slope engineering contractor Fullwealth Holdings (2295) said its controlling stake will change hands after Greensun Global Energy, led by Zhu Liming, agreed to buy 300 million shares, or 75%, from chairman Tse Shing Kei and CEO Ho Ka Kei at HK$0.6545 per share, according to Ming Pao.

The total consideration is more than HK$196 million. The offer price represents a 65.6% discount to the closing price of HK$1.9 before trading was suspended on June 22.

Zhu will then make a mandatory general offer to Fullwealth Holdings at HK$0.6546 per share. The company’s shares are set to resume trading tomorrow morning.
